seo.title: Building Research Agents with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Pydantic AI enables building type-safe research agents that perform multi-step web searches, data synthesis, and structured summarization for production applications. Guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like hallucinated claims, outdated sources, and biased outputs in high-stakes information retrieval tasks. Vex integration provides runtime verification for source accuracy, recency, and factual consistency without disrupting agent workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
Pydantic AI research agents follow a multi-stage pipeline: a router agent dispatches queries to specialized tools for web search, page scraping, and claim extraction. Search tools use APIs like SerpAPI or Tavily, feeding raw content into LLM analyzers that structure data into Pydantic models for claims, sources, and summaries. A synthesis agent validates and compiles results, applying result validators to ensure structured outputs meet schema requirements before final delivery.

The architecture emphasizes dependency injection for tool contexts and streaming validation for real-time feedback. Nested tool calls maintain state via Pydantic's context propagation, while retry logic handles transient API failures. This creates reliable, observable pipelines for production research applications.

Common patterns include agent-tool hierarchies with structured tool responses and multi-turn reasoning chains that accumulate evidence across steps.

risks.name: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
risks.description: Pydantic schemas reject semantically correct responses that deviate from expected formats during streaming synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex result validators with flexible claim verification before strict Pydantic parsing

risks.name: Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
risks.description: Search APIs fail consistently (rate limits, blocks) but retries hide source quality issues
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors tool call success rates and flags persistent failures

risks.name: Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
risks.description: Nested scrapers lose search context, leading to orphaned claims without source attribution
risks.mitigation: Vex propagates context through tool call traces for end-to-end verification

risks.name: Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
risks.description: Partial summaries fail Pydantic validation during token streaming, causing incomplete responses
risks.mitigation: Vex validates streaming chunks incrementally with source verification

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent with Pydantic AI
implementation.description: Define a router agent that orchestrates search and synthesis with structured Pydantic outputs
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List

class Claim(BaseModel):
    text: str
    source_url: str
    recency_score: float = Field(..., ge=0, le=1)

class ResearchResult(BaseModel):
    summary: str
    claims: List[Claim]
    sources: List[str]

@tool
async def web_search(query: str) -> List[str]:
    # Simulated SerpAPI call
    return [f'https://example.com/search?q={query}']

research_agent = Agent(
    'Research Agent',
    model='gpt-4o',
    result_model=ResearchResult,
    tools=[web_search]
)

implementation.title: Multi-Step Claim Extraction Pipeline
implementation.description: Chain search → scrape → extract → verify for comprehensive research workflows
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, tool

@tool
async def scrape_page(url: str) -> str:
    return 'Sample content from ' + url

@tool
async def extract_claims(content: str) -> List[Claim]:
    return [Claim(text='AI improves productivity', source_url='example.com', recency_score=0.8)]

extraction_agent = Agent(
    'Extraction Agent',
    tools=[scrape_page, extract_claims],
    result_model=List[Claim]
)

async def research_pipeline(query: str):
    search_results = await research_agent.run(query)
    claims = await extraction_agent.run(search_results)
    return claims

implementation.title: Streaming Research Results
implementation.description: Enable streaming structured outputs with real-time validation
implementation.code:
async for chunk in research_agent.stream(query, stream=True):
    if chunk.type == 'result_chunk':
        print(f'Partial summary: {chunk.data.summary}')
    elif chunk.type == 'tool_call':
        print(f'Searching: {chunk.data.args.query}')

# Full structured result
result = await research_agent.run(query)
assert isinstance(result, ResearchResult)

implementation.title: Error Handling with Retry Logic
implementation.description: Implement robust tool retry patterns for unreliable search APIs

vexIntegration.title: Add Vex Source Verification Validator
vexIntegration.description: Verify all claims have valid, accessible source URLs before Pydantic parsing
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex, Verifier
from pydantic_ai import Agent

vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-key')
source_verifier = Verifier(
    name='source_accessibility',
    checks=[
        'url_reachable',
        'content_length>100',
        'no_404_errors'
    ]
)

@research_agent.result_validator
async def verify_sources(result: ResearchResult):
    for claim in result.claims:
        await vex.verify(claim.dict(), source_verifier)
    return result

vexIntegration.title: Claim Accuracy with Vex Fact-Checking
vexIntegration.description: Cross-verify extracted claims against source content and web consensus
vexIntegration.code:
claim_accuracy = Verifier(
    name='claim_accuracy',
    checks=[
        'semantic_match>0.8',
        'source_content_alignment',
        'web_consensus_support',
        'temporal_consistency'
    ]
)

@extraction_agent.result_validator
async def check_claims(claims: List[Claim]):
    verified_claims = []
    for claim in claims:
        verdict = await vex.verify(claim.dict(), claim_accuracy)
        if verdict.passed:
            verified_claims.append(claim)
    return verified_claims

vexIntegration.title: Recency and Bias Detection Guardrails
vexIntegration.description: Ensure sources are recent and detect viewpoint bias in research results
vexIntegration.code:
recency_bias_checker = Verifier(
    name='temporal_bias_check',
    checks=[
        'published_date>30_days',
        'author_diversity',
        'domain_neutrality',
        'citation_balance'
    ]
)

async def full_research_with_vex(query: str):
    raw_result = await research_agent.run(query)
    
    # Multi-verifier pipeline
    verifiers = [source_verifier, claim_accuracy, recency_bias_checker]
    
    verified_result = await vex.batch_verify(
        raw_result.dict(), verifiers
    )
    
    return ResearchResult(**verified_result)
